meta.title: Why are there so many different kinds of dogs?
meta.speaker: Kathleen Morrill Pirovich
meta.youtubeTitle: Why are there so many different kinds of dogs? - Kathleen Morrill Pirovich

sentences.text: In the 1500s, there was a little dog called turnspit whose job was to roast meat.
sentences.translation: 在十六世纪,有一种叫做转叉犬的小狗，它们的工作是烤肉。

sentences.text: The dog was a widespread kitchen attendant until the mid-1800s, when new tech took the breed's trade.
sentences.translation: 这种狗曾是常见的厨房帮手， 直到十九世纪中期，新技术取代了它们的工作。

sentences.text: The turnspit was just one of a vast constellation of dog breeds, past and present.
sentences.translation: 转叉犬只是过去和现在 众多犬种中的一种。狗极多样的形态

sentences.text: The immense physical variety of dogs makes them among the world’s most diverse species, and likely the one with the greatest range in size.
sentences.translation: 使它们成为世界上最多样化的物种之一，也大概是体型差异最大的物种。比如，一只博美犬可能重两千克，

sentences.text: A Pomeranian, for example, might weigh two kilograms, while a Mastiff could tip the scale to 100.
sentences.translation: 而一只英国獒犬 可能重达 100 千克。那么，这是为什么呢？ 狗的品种到底意味着什么？

sentences.text: So, why is this?
sentences.translation: 所有狗都是古狼的后裔，

sentences.text: And what does a dog’s breed actually say about them?
sentences.translation: 这些狼至少在 15,000 年前 就开始生活在人类周围。

sentences.text: All dogs descend from ancient wolves that started living around humans at least 15,000 years ago.
sentences.translation: 经过数千年的演变， 狗是第一个被驯化的物种。据信，随着时间的推移， 人类开始依赖狗来完成特定的任务，

sentences.text: Dogs are the first domesticated species, by a landslide of thousands of years.
sentences.translation: 并为特定的特征有选择地繁殖它们，

sentences.text: Over time, it's thought that humans began relying on dogs for specific tasks and selectively breeding them for particular traits, fueling the dramatic diversification of dogs planet-wide.
sentences.translation: 这使全球范围内狗的差异更为巨大。到 11,000 年前， 它们已经呈现出各种各样的外貌。最终，在维多利亚时代的英国， 正式的犬种繁殖开始兴起。

sentences.text: By 11,000 years ago, they’d already assumed an array of appearances.
sentences.translation: 人们不再为了突显功能给狗配种，

sentences.text: And finally, formalized dog breeding took off in Victorian England.
sentences.translation: 而是选择性地让狗近亲繁殖以获得

sentences.text: Instead of mating dogs to emphasize their function, people selectively inbred dogs to achieve particular features and established overarching breed standards.
sentences.translation: 特定的特征，达到既定的整体品种标准。尽管保持特定的遗传线和特征 对养犬俱乐部很重要，但品种遗传学是复杂的。

sentences.text: But while maintaining specific lines of inheritance and characteristics is important to kennel clubs, breed genetics are complex.
sentences.translation: 在一项研究中，科学家测定了 不同犬种的基因序列，发现了 10 个主要的犬类，它们大致 与其世系历史上的工作角色一致——从指示和驱赶到追踪气味和衔回。

sentences.text: In one study where scientists sequenced the genomes of different dog breeds, 10 major groups emerged that roughly aligned with the lineage’s historic working role— from pointing and flushing to scent-tracking and retrieving.
sentences.translation: 科学家可能能够识别 不同品种显著的基因特征，但很难弄清楚这些基因特征 究竟是如何塑造定义品种的特征的。解析狗的品种在多大程度上 影响了它们的性格尤其如此，因为狗的性格是由它们 在一系列场景中一致的行为所定义的。

sentences.text: Within different breeds, scientists may be able to identify distinct genetic signatures, but it's difficult to untangle how those genetic signatures actually shape breed-defining traits.
sentences.translation: 在一项研究中，研究人员比较了 许多不同狗的基因数据，他们发现一只狗约 9% 的 性格特征归因于它的品种。该研究中一些最具遗传性的特征似乎源于长期存在的， 也表现在狼身上的掠食行为。

sentences.text: This is especially true when it comes to parsing how much a dog’s breed informs their personality, as defined by their consistent behaviors across a range of scenarios.
sentences.translation: 这可以从边境牧羊犬如何用眼睛追踪， 金毛寻回犬如何追逐抓咬中看出。其他特征似乎也在这一过程中根深蒂固。例如，像葡萄牙水犬这样的品种 往往感觉把自己弄湿特别舒服；

sentences.text: When researchers in one study compared the genetic data from many different dogs, they found about 9% of a dog’s personality traits were attributed specifically to its breed.
sentences.translation: 寻回犬有取回物品的天赋；而对于哈士奇、雪橇犬和 猎犬之类的狗，长嚎似乎是一种习惯。狗对人类指令的反应能力是另一个 按品种划分最具遗传性的行为特征，边境牧羊犬表现突出。

sentences.text: Some of the traits in the study that were most heritable seemed to be derived from long-standing predatory behaviors also exhibited in wolves.
sentences.translation: 其他行为，包括排便前转圈，以及是否容易被激怒或有攻击性，似乎不是非常有品种特异性。

sentences.text: This is seen in how border collies stalk with their eyes, and how golden retrievers chase and grab-bite.
sentences.translation: 因此，尽管人们普遍认为 斗牛犬更具攻击性，这项研究使人们对这个名声产生了怀疑。

sentences.text: And other traits also seem to have gotten ingrained along the way.
sentences.translation: 玩具品种的确似乎更加独立， 没那么喜欢与其他狗交往，

sentences.text: For example, breeds like Portuguese water dogs do tend to be especially comfortable getting wet; retrievers have an aptitude for fetch; and for dogs like huskies, malamutes, and hounds, howling seems habitual.
sentences.translation: 但可能是非遗传因素在起作用。例如，较小的狗可能 不大会主动与其他狗玩耍，因为它们体型小或是如何被社会化的，不一定是因为它们的基因 如何连接它们大脑的。同一项研究发现，除品种外，

sentences.text: How well a dog responds to human direction was another of the most heritable behavioral traits by breed, with border collies standing out.
sentences.translation: 一只狗的性格由基因决定的不到 25％。因此，遗传有起作用，但绝不是一切。服务犬繁殖项目也说明了这一点，

sentences.text: Other behaviors— including circling before pooping, and how easily provoked or aggressive a dog is— don’t appear to be super breed-specific.
sentences.translation: 这些项目根据狗的性情来繁殖特定的狗。这产生了很多适合服务的狗，但由于个体差异，有些仍然不符合要求。

sentences.text: So, while pit bull terriers are widely considered to be more aggressive dogs, this research casts doubt on their reputation.
sentences.translation: 尽管人类对犬类特征进行了大量研究，但狗的行为是由基因和环境 复杂混合塑造的。

sentences.text: Toy breeds did appear to be more independent and less sociable with other dogs, but non-genetic factors could be at work.
sentences.translation: 这项研究使人们对这个名声产生了怀疑。玩具品种的确似乎更加独立， 没那么喜欢与其他狗交往，但可能是非遗传因素在起作用。例如，较小的狗可能 不大会主动与其他狗玩耍，

sentences.text: For example, smaller dogs might not initiate much play with other dogs because they're small or how they're socialized, and not necessarily due to how their genes wire their brains.
sentences.translation: 但可能是非遗传因素在起作用。例如，较小的狗可能 不大会主动与其他狗玩耍，因为它们体型小或是如何被社会化的，不一定是因为它们的基因 如何连接它们大脑的。同一项研究发现，除品种外，

sentences.text: The same study found that, beyond breed, less than 25% of a dog's personality was accounted for by their genes.
sentences.translation: 不一定是因为它们的基因 如何连接它们大脑的。同一项研究发现，除品种外，一只狗的性格由基因决定的不到 25％。因此，遗传有起作用，但绝不是一切。

sentences.text: So, genetics play a role, but they’re not nearly everything.
sentences.translation: 一只狗的性格由基因决定的不到 25％。因此，遗传有起作用，但绝不是一切。服务犬繁殖项目也说明了这一点，

sentences.text: This is also illustrated in service dog breeding programs, which deliberately breed certain dogs based on their temperament.
sentences.translation: 因此，遗传有起作用，但绝不是一切。服务犬繁殖项目也说明了这一点，这些项目根据狗的性情来繁殖特定的狗。这产生了很多适合服务的狗，

sentences.text: This yields lots of dogs suited for service— but because of individual variation, some still don't make the cut.
sentences.translation: 这些项目根据狗的性情来繁殖特定的狗。这产生了很多适合服务的狗，但由于个体差异，有些仍然不符合要求。尽管人类对犬类特征进行了大量研究，

sentences.text: As much as humans have heavily tinkered with canine characteristics, dog behavior is shaped by a complex mix of their genes and environment.
sentences.translation: 但由于个体差异，有些仍然不符合要求。尽管人类对犬类特征进行了大量研究，但狗的行为是由基因和环境 复杂混合塑造的。

sentences.text: And breed alone appears to be a pretty poor proxy for all that makes up the personality of a given pup.
sentences.translation: 但狗的行为是由基因和环境 复杂混合塑造的。鉴于所有这些影响因素，仅凭品种 来判定某只小狗的性格似乎很不可靠。

cues.text: In the 1500s, there was a little dog called turnspit
whose job was to roast meat.
The dog was a widespread kitchen attendant until the mid-1800s,
when new tech took the breed's trade.
The turnspit was just one of a vast constellation of dog breeds,
past and present.
The immense physical variety of dogs
makes them among the world’s most diverse species,
and likely the one with the greatest range in size.
A Pomeranian, for example, might weigh two kilograms,
while a Mastiff could tip the scale to 100.
So, why is this?
And what does a dog’s breed actually say about them?
All dogs descend from ancient wolves
that started living around humans at least 15,000 years ago.
Dogs are the first domesticated species, by a landslide of thousands of years.
Over time, it's thought that humans began relying on dogs for specific tasks
and selectively breeding them for particular traits,
fueling the dramatic diversification of dogs planet-wide.
By 11,000 years ago, they’d already assumed an array of appearances.
And finally, formalized dog breeding took off in Victorian England.
Instead of mating dogs to emphasize their function,
people selectively inbred dogs to achieve particular features
and established overarching breed standards.
But while maintaining specific lines of inheritance and characteristics
is important to kennel clubs,
breed genetics are complex.
In one study where scientists sequenced the genomes of different dog breeds,
10 major groups emerged that roughly aligned
with the lineage’s historic working role—
from pointing and flushing to scent-tracking and retrieving.
Within different breeds,
scientists may be able to identify distinct genetic signatures,
but it's difficult to untangle how those genetic signatures
actually shape breed-defining traits.
This is especially true
when it comes to parsing how much a dog’s breed informs their personality,
as defined by their consistent behaviors across a range of scenarios.
When researchers in one study compared the genetic data
from many different dogs,
they found about 9% of a dog’s personality traits
were attributed specifically to its breed.
Some of the traits in the study that were most heritable
seemed to be derived from long-standing predatory behaviors
also exhibited in wolves.
This is seen in how border collies stalk with their eyes,
and how golden retrievers chase and grab-bite.
And other traits also seem to have gotten ingrained along the way.
For example, breeds like Portuguese water dogs
do tend to be especially comfortable getting wet;
retrievers have an aptitude for fetch;
and for dogs like huskies, malamutes, and hounds,
howling seems habitual.
How well a dog responds to human direction
was another of the most heritable behavioral traits by breed,
with border collies standing out.
Other behaviors— including circling before pooping,
and how easily provoked or aggressive a dog is—
don’t appear to be super breed-specific.
So, while pit bull terriers are widely considered to be more aggressive dogs,
this research casts doubt on their reputation.
Toy breeds did appear to be more independent
and less sociable with other dogs,
but non-genetic factors could be at work.
For example, smaller dogs might not initiate much play with other dogs
because they're small or how they're socialized,
and not necessarily due to how their genes wire their brains.
The same study found that, beyond breed,
less than 25% of a dog's personality was accounted for by their genes.
So, genetics play a role, but they’re not nearly everything.
This is also illustrated in service dog breeding programs,
which deliberately breed certain dogs based on their temperament.
This yields lots of dogs suited for service—
but because of individual variation, some still don't make the cut.
As much as humans have heavily tinkered with canine characteristics,
dog behavior is shaped by a complex mix of their genes and environment.
And breed alone appears to be a pretty poor proxy
for all that makes up the personality of a given pup.

cuesZh.text: 在十六世纪,有一种叫做转叉犬的小狗，
它们的工作是烤肉。
这种狗曾是常见的厨房帮手， 直到十九世纪中期，
新技术取代了它们的工作。
转叉犬只是过去和现在 众多犬种中的一种。
狗极多样的形态
使它们成为世界上最多样化的物种之一，
也大概是体型差异最大的物种。
比如，一只博美犬可能重两千克，
而一只英国獒犬 可能重达 100 千克。
那么，这是为什么呢？ 狗的品种到底意味着什么？
所有狗都是古狼的后裔，
这些狼至少在 15,000 年前 就开始生活在人类周围。
经过数千年的演变， 狗是第一个被驯化的物种。
据信，随着时间的推移， 人类开始依赖狗来完成特定的任务，
并为特定的特征有选择地繁殖它们，
这使全球范围内狗的差异更为巨大。
到 11,000 年前， 它们已经呈现出各种各样的外貌。
最终，在维多利亚时代的英国， 正式的犬种繁殖开始兴起。
人们不再为了突显功能给狗配种，
而是选择性地让狗近亲繁殖以获得
特定的特征，达到既定的整体品种标准。
尽管保持特定的遗传线和特征 对养犬俱乐部很重要，
但品种遗传学是复杂的。
在一项研究中，科学家测定了 不同犬种的基因序列，
发现了 10 个主要的犬类，它们大致 与其世系历史上的工作角色一致——
从指示和驱赶到追踪气味和衔回。
科学家可能能够识别 不同品种显著的基因特征，
但很难弄清楚这些基因特征 究竟是如何塑造定义品种的特征的。
解析狗的品种在多大程度上 影响了它们的性格尤其如此，
因为狗的性格是由它们 在一系列场景中一致的行为所定义的。
在一项研究中，研究人员比较了 许多不同狗的基因数据，
他们发现一只狗约 9% 的 性格特征归因于它的品种。
该研究中一些最具遗传性的特征
似乎源于长期存在的， 也表现在狼身上的掠食行为。
这可以从边境牧羊犬如何用眼睛追踪， 金毛寻回犬如何追逐抓咬中看出。
其他特征似乎也在这一过程中根深蒂固。
例如，像葡萄牙水犬这样的品种 往往感觉把自己弄湿特别舒服；
寻回犬有取回物品的天赋；
而对于哈士奇、雪橇犬和 猎犬之类的狗，长嚎似乎是一种习惯。
狗对人类指令的反应能力是另一个 按品种划分最具遗传性的行为特征，
边境牧羊犬表现突出。
其他行为，包括排便前转圈，
以及是否容易被激怒或有攻击性，
似乎不是非常有品种特异性。
因此，尽管人们普遍认为 斗牛犬更具攻击性，
这项研究使人们对这个名声产生了怀疑。
玩具品种的确似乎更加独立， 没那么喜欢与其他狗交往，
但可能是非遗传因素在起作用。
例如，较小的狗可能 不大会主动与其他狗玩耍，
因为它们体型小或是如何被社会化的，
不一定是因为它们的基因 如何连接它们大脑的。
同一项研究发现，除品种外，
一只狗的性格由基因决定的不到 25％。
因此，遗传有起作用，但绝不是一切。
服务犬繁殖项目也说明了这一点，
这些项目根据狗的性情来繁殖特定的狗。
这产生了很多适合服务的狗，
但由于个体差异，有些仍然不符合要求。
尽管人类对犬类特征进行了大量研究，
但狗的行为是由基因和环境 复杂混合塑造的。
鉴于所有这些影响因素，仅凭品种 来判定某只小狗的性格似乎很不可靠。
